(CCP 430.10(B), (E) & (G).) FOR COUNT ONE, CONSPIRACY IS NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION. (APPLIED EQUIPMENT CORP. V. LITTON SAUDI ARABIA LTD. (1994) 7 CAL.4TH 503, 514.) P LAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE THE CONSPIRACY ALLEGATIONS AS GENERAL ALLEGATIONS. FOR COUNT TWO, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT ALLEGED WHETHER THE CONTRACT IS WRITTEN, ORAL, OR IMPLIED BY CONDUCT. (CCP 430.10(G).) FOR COUNT THREE, ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INVOLUNTARY DISSOLUTION (CCP 430.10(E)), PLAINTIFF SAFIEH HAS NOT CLEARLY ALLEGED IN WHAT CAPACITY SHE IS BRINGING THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. ATTY. D. INDER COMAR OF THE COMAR LAW, TEL. NO. 562-6790 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT FOOD & LIQUOR WORLD, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FOOD & LIQUOR WORLD, INCORPORATED'S Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff Mehran Golchehreh And Related Claims Due To Lack Of Standing is denied without prejudice. The filed motion relies on extrinsic evidence, rather than being based on the face of the complaint. The motion thus cannot be construed as a demurrer or a judgment on the pleadings. If the Court were to treat the motion as a motion for summary judgment, it does not meet the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure section 437c. (See 5 Witkin, Cal. Procedure (5th ed. 2008) Pleading, 1004-1006, pp. 412-418; Pianka v. California (1956) Cal. 2d 208.). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Disqualify Fred K Sepapour From Representing Mehran Golchehreh And Safieh Golchehreh Adverse To Deft'S In This Action
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FOOD & LIQUOR WORLD, INC.'S MOTION To Disqualify Fred K Sepapour From Representing Mehran Golchehreh And Safieh Golchehreh Adverse To Defendants In This Action is granted. Counsel Fred K. Sepapour is disqualified from representing Plaintiffs in this action. Absent informed written consent, a lawyer may not represent a client in litigation against a former client that bears a "substantial relationship" to the earlier proceedings. City & County of San Francisco v. Cobra Solutions, Inc. (2006) 38 Cal.4th 839, 847. A "substantial relationship" exists whenever the subject matter of the prior and the current representations are linked in some rational manner. Flatt v. Sup. Ct. (Daniel) (1994) 9 Cal.4the 275, 283. The moving party must disclose detailed facts showing the acquisition of confidential information. Faughn v. Perez (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 592, 601. The court finds that a conflict exists. Counsel formerly represented Defendant Food & Liquor as to its incorporation and counsel likely obtained confidential information. Moreover, it is clear that counsel had an ongoing relationship with several of the family members who are engaged in this matter and are adverse to one another. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SAEED GOLCHEHREH, DAVID GOLCHEHREH, SUZANNE GOLCHEHREH AND SEPEDAH GOLCHEHREH'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained, with 30 days leave to amend, the demurrer as to Count One, Count Two, and Count Three. (CCP 430.10(b), (e) & (g).) For Count One, conspiracy is not a cause of action. (Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 514.) P laintiffs are granted leave to amend to allege the conspiracy allegations as general allegations. For Count Two, Plaintiffs have not alleged whether the contract is written, oral, or implied by conduct. (CCP 430.10(g).) For Count Three, although Plaintiff has sufficiently pled a cause of action for involuntary dissolution (CCP 430.10(e)), Plaintiff Safieh has not clearly alleged in what capacity she is bringing this cause of action. (CCP 430.10(b).) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FOOD & LIQUOR WORLD, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained, with 30 days leave to amend, as the entire complaint for uncertainty and for failure to plead sufficient facts as to Count One and Count Two. (CCP 430.10(e), (f) & (g).) Overruled as to the demurrer of the entire complaint for lack of capacity and misjoinder and for failure to plead facts as to Count Three. (CCP 430.10(a) & (d).) Plaintiff Safieh's statements do not amount to facts sufficient for the Court to determine that Plaintiff Safieh lacks the legal capacity to sue. (See Prob. Code 810, 811.) The Complaint lacks designations for each count pursuant to CRC 2.112, and Plaintiffs therefore fail to inform Defendant which claims are being asserted against it. Misjoinder of parties is not present on the face of this Complaint. (CCP 379.) For Count One, conspiracy is not a cause of action. (Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 514.) Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend to allege the conspiracy allegations as general allegations. For Count Two, Plaintiffs have not alleged whether the contract is written, oral, or implied by conduct. (CCP 430.10(g).) For Count Three, an insufficient verification is not a proper grounds for demurrer. (Butterfield v. Graves (1902) 138 Cal. 155, 158.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
